answer:
The Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for Slack enables AI assistants to interact with your workspace by providing tools to search messages, retrieve channel history, send messages, and access user profiles [1][2].

There are two primary approaches to implementation:

Official Slack-approved integrations require creating a Slack App, obtaining OAuth tokens, and often require admin approval [1][3][4]. These are designed for secure, authorized access by third-party AI assistants like Claude or Perplexity [1].

Community-developed servers (such as those by korotovsky, jtalk22, or karbassi) offer alternative, session-based implementations [5][6][7]. These often bypass the need for formal app registration or admin approval by using browser session tokens, allowing the AI to act with the same permissions as your user account [5][7].
